pr.title: gr: fix Zebes Route param store
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Fixed the Zebes Route stage parameter global by modeling `grZe_Route_804D6A60` as an 8-byte parameter store containing a params pointer plus padding, instead of as the params pointer itself. The PR also named the currently understood route params, `camera_timer` and `zako_spawn_chance`, and brought `grzebesroute` data sections including `.sbss` to 100% while leaving known function/TU fuzzy percentages unchanged.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; adds Zebes Route params/store structs and updates all uses of `grZe_Route_804D6A60`.

what_changed: Added `grZebesRoute_Params` with two named integer fields: `camera_timer` and `zako_spawn_chance`.
Added `grZebesRoute_ParamStore`, an 8-byte wrapper containing `grZebesRoute_Params* params` plus `u32 pad`.
Changed `grZe_Route_804D6A60` from a static anonymous pointer with fields `x0` and `x4` into a concrete `grZebesRoute_ParamStore` global.
Changed `grZebesRoute_8020B164` to store `Ground_801C49F8()` into `grZe_Route_804D6A60.params` rather than assigning the global itself.
Changed `grZebesRoute_8020B1F4` to use `grZe_Route_804D6A60.params->zako_spawn_chance` for the Zako generator random bound.
The PR body reports `grzebesroute` data sections are now 100%; match notes say TU fuzzy remains 99.98663% and `grZebesRoute_8020B548` remains 99.96923%.
smart_moves: Separated the pointed-to stage params from the global storage wrapper, which reflects the apparent `.sbss` layout better than treating `grZe_Route_804D6A60` as the pointer itself.
Used an explicit `u32 pad` field to account for the 8-byte store without inventing semantics for the second word.
Named params from observed usage: the first field feeds `gp->gv.zebes2.xC4` as a timer-like value, and the second is passed to `HSD_Randi` around Zako spawning logic.
Targeted data-section correctness without perturbing unrelated fuzzy-matching function work.
decomp_lessons: If an `.sbss` symbol looks like a pointer but data matching is off by a word, test whether the real object is a small store struct containing a pointer plus padding or metadata.
For stage parameter globals, keep the storage object and the pointed-to parameter block as distinct types when assignments come from helpers such as `Ground_801C49F8()`.
Use callsite behavior to replace anonymous `x0`/`x4` fields with semantic names, but leave unknown words as `pad` when evidence is weak.
A PR can legitimately improve data-section matching to 100% even when known TU/function fuzzy mismatches remain unresolved; document both outcomes.
naming_conventions: Stage-local structs follow the existing `grZebesRoute_*` prefix, e.g. `grZebesRoute_Params` and `grZebesRoute_ParamStore` alongside `grZebesRoute_LightData`.
Understood parameter fields use descriptive snake_case: `camera_timer`, `zako_spawn_chance`.
The address-based global name `grZe_Route_804D6A60` was retained while its type was improved.
Unknown storage words should be named plainly as padding, e.g. `pad`, rather than given speculative gameplay names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Verification commands from the PR body: `ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.o build/GALE01/report.json` and full `ninja`.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched data at 39.36% with +8 bytes, and one new match for `main/melee/gr/grzebesroute` `.sbss`: +4 bytes, 50.00% to 100.00%.
The key matching tactic was correcting the global symbol size/layout in `.sbss` by replacing a pointer global with an 8-byte store struct.
Do not expect this change alone to fix `grZebesRoute_8020B548`; the PR explicitly notes that function remains 99.96923% fuzzy.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback confirmed the `.sbss` improvement for `main/melee/gr/grzebesroute` from 50.00% to 100.00%.
searchable_terms: gr: fix Zebes Route param store
follow_up_queries: Search other stage files for globals assigned from `Ground_801C49F8()` that are modeled as raw pointers but may actually be small param-store structs.
Investigate whether the `u32 pad` in `grZebesRoute_ParamStore` has real semantics or is only layout padding.
Look for external stage parameter tables or sibling stages that confirm the names `camera_timer` and `zako_spawn_chance`.
Continue work on `grZebesRoute_8020B548`, which this PR leaves at 99.96923% fuzzy.
Consider whether `gp->gv.zebes2.xC4` can be renamed or typed using the new `camera_timer` evidence.
